title: sideritis grandiflora var. baetica
intro: Sideritis grandiflora var. baetica is a subspecies of the Sideritis grandiflora plant, commonly known as the Spanish ironwort or Baetic mountain tea, which is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sideritis grandiflora var. baetica include reducing inflammation, fighting free radicals, and potentially improving cardiovascular health, although further research is needed to confirm its effects on human health. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, or dried and used in capsules or tinctures, although the optimal preparation method and dosage for medicinal use remain unclear.
